items.title: Heerenveen gaat voor nieuwe bekerstunt: 'Laten we nog maar eens verrassen'
items.summary: De bekerwedstrijd van woensdagavond tegen PSV zou weleens cruciaal kunnen zijn voor de Europese ambities van SC Heerenveen. Middenvelder Marcus Linday kijkt uit naar een ongetwijfeld zwaar avondje in Eindhoven.

items.title: Koopmeiners glundert na bekerzege en kijkt uit naar Ajax: 'Extra vertrouwen tanken'
items.summary: In de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker trok AZ tegen FC Twente aan het langste eind en heeft daarmee het ticket naar de halve finale in handen. Na afloop sprak verslaggever Kalum van Oudheusden met Peer Koopmeiners, die zijn lach niet van zijn gezicht kreeg.

items.title: Testing protocol for prostate cancer 'alarming' - Hislop
items.summary: Shaka Hislop says it is "alarming" that tests for prostate cancer are not "regular and "standardised" in the United Kingdom.

items.title: 'Vader' Flick gelukkig voor Araújo na geslaagde basisrentree bij Barça
items.summary: Barcelona-trainer Hansi Flick was dinsdagavond erg te spreken over de rol die Ronald Araújo vervulde bij de bekerzege van Barcelona op Albacete. De Uruguayaanse verdediger stond na een periode waarin hij kampte mentale problemen weer in de basis bij Barça en was ook direct trefzeker.

items.title: AZ en Twente zorgen voor echt bekervoetbal: 'Avond vol emotie en spektakel'
items.summary: AZ had in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker een verlenging nodig om FC Twente op de knieën te krijgen. Hoewel de Tukkers de meeste kansen bij elkaar speelden, was het Troy Parrott die voor de beslissing zorgde en zijn ploeg naar de halve finale schoot. Clubwatchers Simon Zwartkruis en Geert-Jan Jakobs over de bekerwedstrijd met het venijn in de staart.

items.title: Transfers Ajax: de vele pogingen die Ajax deed voor een '6'
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Ajax op de voet, ook nu de winterse transferwindow gesloten is. De doelwitten van de Amsterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Supreme George seeks four-timer at Sedgefield
items.summary: We have a busy day's racing on Wednesday with action across both codes from Newcastle and Sedgefield, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: T20 World Cup Group A: Defending champions India the team to beat
items.summary: We look at the sides in Group A - India, Pakistan, USA, Netherlands and Namibia - for the T20 World Cup, which is live on Sky Sports from February 7 to March 8.

items.title: Araujo hugs Flick after goal in 'step by step' return
items.summary: Hansi Flick said Barcelona will continue to support Ronald Araújo after the defender netted on his first start for the club since returning from a mental health break against Albacete in the Copa del Rey on Tuesday.

items.title: Kante's move to Fenerbahce back on after collapse
items.summary: World Cup winner N'Golo Kanté's move to Fenerbahce is set to be finalized after Al-Ittihad finally agreed to let him go on Tuesday.

items.title: Lens condemn Allan Saint-Maximin racist abuse
items.summary: Ligue 1 side Lens have released a statement in support of new signing Allan Saint-Maximin after racist comments were made under his signing announcement.

items.title: Arteta: 'Magical' final is 'best vitamins' for Arsenal
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has described Arsenal 's "magical" achievement in reaching the Carabao Cup Final as "the best vitamins we can put in our bodies."

items.title: WSL deadline day: Arsenal sign keeper Votikova on loan, Kramzar joins Everton
items.summary: Barbora Votikova signs to provide necessary cover Chelsea offer for Jennifer Echegini rejected by PSG Arsenal signed the Czech Republic goalkeeper Barbora Votikova on loan from Slavia Prague, while Everton completed a move for the Slovenia midfielder Zara Kramzar from Roma late on the Women’s Super League’s transfer deadline day. The former Tottenham Hotspur and Paris Saint-Germain player will provide valuable cover in goal, with Manuela Zinsberger having suffered an anterior cruciate ligament injury in October and Anneke Borbe being stretchered off during Sunday’s Champions Cup final victory over Corinthians following a collision. Borbe was discharged from hospital later that day. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Years in the making' - are Arsenal ready to shed 'nearly-men' tag?
items.summary: Arsenal have moved a step closer to winning their first major silverware since 2020 by beating Chelsea 4-2 on aggregate in the Carabao Cup semi-final.

items.title: Redknapp: Arsenal can win two or three trophies
items.summary: Jamie Redknapp backed Arsenal to go on and win "two or three" trophies after they beat Chelsea to reach the Carabao Cup final on a night described as "magical" by manager Mikel Arteta.

items.title: Papers: Ronaldo considers European return amid Al Nassr 'strike'
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Wednesday's newspapers...

items.title: Chelsea adopt Match of the Day tactic to stop Arsenal set-piece threat
items.summary: Despite losing their Carabao Cup semi-final, Chelsea attempted a new way of stopping Arsenal set-pieces - just days after a similar suggestion on Match of the Day.

items.title: Hearts’ title hopes hit by late St Mirren winner after Halkett’s costly red card
items.summary: Craig Halkett’s first-half red card proved costly for the Scottish Premiership leaders Hearts as they lost 1-0 at St Mirren after a late header from Miguel Freckleton. The centre-back was dismissed in the 29th minute for denying the Saints debutant Jacob Devaney a goalscoring opportunity. It was the third time in five league games that Hearts had been reduced to 10 men. Unlike the previous two occasions, however, they were unable to grind out a result in Paisley as Freckleton’s 88th-minute header consigned them to a third defeat of the season. The result leaves Hearts six points ahead of Celtic and Rangers, both of whom play their games in hand on Wednesday. Continue reading...

items.title: Kai Havertz adds killer touch as Arsenal hold off Chelsea to book final place
items.summary: When the final whistle blew, a huge wave of relief swept over this stadium. Arsenal may not have been at all convincing against a Chelsea side who caused them the occasional problem but Mikel Arteta will not care one bit. After four successive semi-final defeats, Arsenal are on their way to Wembley. At last. After the drama of the first leg at Stamford Bridge that saw Liam Rosenior’s side travel to north London needing to overturn a 3-2 deficit, this was a much more cagey affair that was eventually settled in stoppage time by Kai Havertz’s breakaway goal against his former club. Arsenal had seemed content to keep Chelsea at arm’s length for most of the evening and rarely looked like extending their advantage until then. Continue reading...

items.title: Liverpool to rival Man Utd for Wharton - Wednesday's gossip
items.summary: Liverpool, Manchester United and Real Madrid eye Adam Wharton deal, Manchester United could move for Jean-Philippe Mateta and Sandro Tonali, Leeds keeper Illan Meslier looks to be heading to Turkey.
